title: cordiera myrciifolia var. barbata
intro: Cordiera myrciifolia var. barbata, also known as the "Santa Cruz root" or "Yage", is a plant native to the Amazon rainforest, specifically in Peru, and is a member of the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cordiera myrciifolia var. barbata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an anticonvulsant and anxiolytic agent. Cordiera myrciifolia var. barbata is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used in traditional medicine in the Amazon region.
